subject: [DCAE][DCAEMOD] Deploy R7 container images
commit_message:
[DCAE][DCAEMOD] Deploy R7 container images

Deploy new versions of cm-container, k8s-bootstrap-container
and healthcheck-container for DCAE.
Deploy a healthcheck-container for DCAEMOD.
Add readiness checks for aaf-cm for dcae-cloudify-manager,
dcae-inventory-api, and dcae-config-binding-service, to
avoid crash loops in the init phase.

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2099
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1789
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2236
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1864
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflucas@research.att.com>
Change-Id: I473a0cd0e3e00d39e6c33d87781581ec527fbc89

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

Hello,
some comments but I like the direction taken.

I would have "cut" this proposal in 2 (one for DCAE, one for DCAEmod)

Since end of frankfurt, we mandate the commit message to follow the following pattern, can you change yours please?

[NAME_OF_COMPONENT|DOC|COMMON|GENERIC] Meaningful title (from OOM side)

at least one sentence explaining the change done in this patch, cause and consequences and possibly more of course

Issue-ID: AS_WE_ARE_FORCED_BUT_MEANINGLESS
Change-ID: xxx
Sign-off: xxx

(so mainly put the component name in front, rest is OK IMHO)

5gbulk is using DCAE and it seems we had a failure with DCAE discussion:

https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//109470-4/xtesting-smoke-usecases-robot/5gbulkpm/5gbulkpm/log.html#s1-t8

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> 5gbulk is using DCAE and it seems we had a failure with DCAE discussion:
> 
> https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//109470-4/xtesting-smoke-usecases-robot/5gbulkpm/5gbulkpm/log.html#s1-t8
> 
> let's oom_redeploy to be sure

The root cause here is that the Cloudify Manager API begins to return 500 status codes to the API requests that the bootstrap container makes to deploy the various DCAE service components. In this case, the dcae-ves-collector needed by the 5gbulk PM was not deployed.

We have seen this problem occasionally in the past, but only in the gating environment.

> The root cause here is that the Cloudify Manager API begins to return 500 status codes to the API requests that the bootstrap container makes to deploy the various DCAE service components. In this case, the dcae-ves-collector needed by the 5gbulk PM was not deployed.
> 
> We have seen this problem occasionally in the past, but only in the gating environment.

hey Jack,
I did took a look on recent deployments of our dailies from Orange and DT and that's true it seems we do not have such error.

This happens only on gating but more and more often.

The thing is our (Orange) dailies (Master and Frankfurt) and the gating are using the exact same code for deploying on the same OpenStack :S

the only difference is that VM and Kubernernetes are "fresh" on dailies whereas we first remove previous onap + /dockerdata-nfs cleaning + namespace removal on our gating.

Is there a way to debug more this because two third of our issues on healtcheck are coming from this?

I am trying to work out how we might capture more information.  If the problem occurred in my local test environment (or in the Wind River environment), I could exec onto the CM container and explore the various logs for clues.   There are a number of components running in the container (breaking it up into proper microservices is on the Cloudify roadmap but not in the near future) with a number of logs in different locations.  Would it be possible to get an archive of the /var/log directory tree from an instance where the problem has occurred?
